key_metrics: Gross margin expanded to 21.1% from 16.3% YoY
Automotive sales revenue grew 19.7% YoY to $15.47 billion
Energy generation and storage revenue declined 11.8% YoY to $2.41 billion
Operating expenses surged 37.2% YoY to $3.78 billion
qoq_changes: Inventory increased 16.5% to $14.43 billion from $12.39 billion at year-end 2025
Property, plant and equipment grew by $2.57 billion to $43.21 billion
Total assets increased to $143.72 billion from $137.81 billion
new_risks: Ongoing supply chain constraints impacting production capacity
Intensifying competition in the EV and energy markets
Significant inventory build-up ($14.4B) may indicate demand softening or logistical bottlenecks
management_tone: Cautiously optimistic; while top-line growth and gross margin expansion demonstrate strong core execution, management highlights ongoing supply chain constraints and competitive pressures.
analyst_note: TSLA delivered a solid quarter with 15.8% revenue growth and impressive gross margin expansion, but a 37% spike in operating expenses and a $2 billion inventory build warrant close monitoring for potential demand or operational headwinds.
